{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"**Findings**

| Decision | Findings | Severity / agent_risk | Justification |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Block merge** | `crime_00001` `lib/command.js` large file | high / `0.70` | 2,179-line command core with 150 top-level functions is the main behavioral surface. On an empty-baseline PR, this is too much coupled logic to merge without decomposition or a clear ownership map. |
| **Block merge** | `crime_00002` `typings/index.d.ts` large file | high / `0.70` | 891-line public type surface with 121 top-level declarations. Type API drift will be hard for agents to edit safely. |
| **Block merge** | `crime_00003` `_executeSubCommand` | high / `0.69` | 120-line core command-execution method. This is high-risk orchestration logic and should be split or heavily tested before merge. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00020`, `00039`, `00040` core large functions | medium / `0.61`, `0.60`, `0.59` | `parseOptions`, `_parseCommand`, and `addOption` are important but below the high-risk cutoff. Worth filing follow-up refactors, especially around parsing behavior. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00041`, `00042`, `00043` large files | medium / `0.59`, `0.59`, `0.57` | `typings/index.test-d.ts`, `lib/help.js`, and `lib/option.js` exceed the file threshold. Not immediate blockers, but they raise future edit risk. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00005`-`00008` production duplicate blocks | medium / `0.62` each | Repeated `argument`/`option` behavior is real maintenance risk. One bug fix could easily land in only one copy. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00004` example duplicate `myParseInt` | medium / `0.62` | Same helper copied between examples. Acceptable in examples unless examples are treated as tested product surface. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00009`-`00019` test duplicate blocks | medium / `0.47` each | Test helper duplication is noisy but not merge-blocking. Consolidate only if it improves test readability. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00021`-`00028` no-assert exit override tests | medium / `0.46` each | These test names claim thrown errors but contain zero assertions. That can give false confidence around CLI exit behavior. Add observable assertions. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00029`-`00036` no-assert TS import tests | medium / `0.46` each | If these are compile-smoke tests, they may be intentional, but the intent should be encoded clearly or asserted where possible. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00037` large test callback | medium / `0.45` | Large test body only. Refactor opportunistically, not a merge blocker. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00038` magic literal `PORT` | medium / `0.60` | Repeated domain literal across examples and typings can become duplicated policy. Warn, but not enough alone to block. |
| **Warn** | `crime_00044`, `00045` broken docs links | low / `0.51` each | Low severity, but broken docs links are cheap to fix and can mislead agents/users. Should be corrected before or soon after merge. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00046` commented-out code in test | low / `0.36` | Dead comment in a test helper area. Clean up when nearby, but not review-blocking. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00047`-`00050` large test suites | low / `0.35`, `0.35`, `0.34`, `0.34` | Test organization smell only. Does not materially change product behavior risk. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00051`-`00059` weak assertions | low / `0.34` each | Weak assertions in tests are worth improving, but low severity and localized. Track as test-quality debt. |
| **Ignore** | `crime_00060` prose rule in comment | low / `0.31` | The comment may carry stale intent, but confidence and risk are low. Not a merge blocker. |

**Merge Call**

I would **block the PR** on the three high findings: `lib/command.js`, `typings/index.d.ts`, and `_executeSubCommand`. The rest are warnings or cleanup debt.

Minimum path to unblock: split or document boundaries for `lib/command.js`, extract `_executeSubCommand` responsibilities, and either split the type declarations or add a deliberate rationale plus focused type tests. Then address the no-assert tests as a follow-up before relying on this suite for agent-assisted changes."}}
